posts.hook: Ophthalmologist explains: why your eyes twitch and what it actually means.
posts.caption:
Why do your eyes twitch?

An ophthalmologist explains:

- Fatigue and stress (most common)
- Caffeine overstimulation
- Screen-induced strain on the orbicularis oculi muscle
- Rarely: neurological conditions (see a doctor if persistent)

95% of the time it's harmless. But it's your body telling you to rest your eyes.

#EyeTwitch #Ophthalmologist #Axeo #EyeHealth #HealthTok #MedTok

posts.hook: We compared every free eye test online. Here's which ones are actually legit.
posts.caption:
We tested every free online vision test we could find.

Most are ad-filled, outdated, or medically questionable.

What makes a vision test legit:
- Based on standardized charts (Snellen, Ishihara)
- Calibrated for screen distance
- No upsell before results

Axeo's tests: free, standardized, instant results.

posts.hook: Ophthalmologist explains the one exercise that actually reduces eye strain.
posts.caption:
Ophthalmologist explains: the one exercise clinically shown to reduce digital eye strain.

It's called Focus Shifting.

Hold your finger 6 inches from your nose. Focus on it for 5 seconds. Then focus on something 20 feet away for 5 seconds. Repeat 10 times.

This trains your ciliary muscle to switch between near and distance focus.

Free guided version at axeo.vision/exercise

#Ophthalmologist #EyeExercise #Axeo #FocusShift #EyeStrain

posts.hook: Interactive Ishihara test — curious how many plates people here can pass.
posts.caption:
Hey r/eyehealth — I've been reading about color vision deficiency and found a free browser-based Ishihara test that's pretty well-made.

Took it myself and passed all plates, but I'm curious:

- How many plates do most people pass?
- Is it common to fail 1-2 plates and still have 'normal' color vision?
- Has anyone here discovered they were color deficient through an online test?

The test I used: axeo.vision/test (free, no signup, instant results)

Would love to hear your experiences or any insights from professionals here.
